summary: Tested Deropo webhook name-extraction fix (BUG-1) and Pipeline UI scroll/rename/smoothing fixes (BUG-2/3). All 6 backend pytest cases pass covering pushname, sender_name, nested contact.name, placeholder auto-upgrade, fallback WA-XXXX, and PUT rename regression. Frontend scroll containers on all 3 columns have correct `overflow-y-auto min-h-0 scroll-smooth overscroll-contain` classes and actually scroll when content exceeds height. Framer-motion 0.18s transitions confirmed on chat bubbles. Chat-messages container has scrollBehavior:smooth. Rename button/input works end-to-end for a lead that exists in the leads collection (PUT /api/leads/{id} fires, toast shown, name updates).

ui_bugs.issue: Rename silently no-ops when a conversation has no matching lead in `leads` collection (only a `wa_conversations` row exists). `renameCustomer` early-returns because `activeItem.lead_id` is undefined. UX-wise the input closes without any error toast, so the user thinks the rename saved. Reproduce: delete a lead but leave its wa_conversation → click that row in pipeline → try rename → nothing happens, no error.

issues: Some leads created historically show `Priya Kumari` name but `notes` field contains raw JSON payload dump (e.g. `{conversation: ..., messageContextInfo: {...}}`). Should be scrubbed to just the human message before storing in notes. Cosmetic — not part of this bug fix scope.

action_items: OPTIONAL/MEDIUM: In `renameCustomer` in `/app/frontend/src/pages/Pipeline.jsx`, when `activeItem?.lead_id` is missing, either (a) auto-create a lead first with the new name + mobile, or (b) show a toast like 'Cannot rename — no lead attached'. Currently it silently does nothing which is confusing.
OPTIONAL/COSMETIC: Sanitize `notes` field when auto-creating a lead from webhook so we don't store raw serialized JSON of message metadata. Some existing leads have unreadable notes.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend fix (deropo.py parse_webhook) is clean — broad set of name aliases + nested contact.name lookup covers real Deropo payload variations.
whatsapp.py `_ensure_lead` auto-upgrade guard uses `startswith('WA ') and len<=8` — safe against overwriting legit names.
Frontend flex scroll fix is textbook correct: `min-h-0` on flex children plus `overflow-y-auto scroll-smooth overscroll-contain` — verified all three columns scroll independently.
Framer-motion transition duration 0.18s confirmed on `AnimatePresence` message bubbles — feels smooth.
Minor: `EditableName` silent no-op when lead_id missing (see action item).

success_rate.frontend: 95% (rename + scroll + smoothing all confirmed working; one minor UX guard suggestion)

seed_data_creation: Backend tests created leads for 919000000111, 222, 333, 444, 555, 666 and cleaned them up via DELETE /api/leads/{id} in fixture teardown. UI test renamed Priya Kumari lead and reverted the name back to 'Priya Kumari' — no lingering test data in leads.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend name-extraction fix fully verified via pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_deropo_name_fix.py (6/6 pass). Frontend scroll classes verified via runtime style probe (scrollHeight>clientHeight, overflowY:auto). Rename works only when a lead row exists — if a wa_conversation exists without a matching lead, rename silently no-ops. Deropo webhook does NOT require auth; /api/leads and /api/whatsapp/conversations require Bearer token from POST /api/auth/login. Frontend token stored in localStorage key `rcb_token`.
rca of the issue: BUG-1 root cause: previous parse_webhook only looked at `data.name`/`data.profile_name` — Deropo actually sends `pushname`, `sender_name`, or nested `contact.name` variants. Fix broadens the alias list and adds nested contact.name lookup, plus `_ensure_lead` now upgrades placeholder `WA XXXX` names in-place when a later webhook arrives with a real profile_name. BUG-2 root cause: flexbox scroll trap — parent flex children didn't have `min-h-0`, so overflow-y-auto never kicked in. Fixed by adding `min-h-0` to each of the 3 columns and their inner scroll containers, plus `scroll-smooth overscroll-contain`. BUG-3: framer-motion 0.18s transitions + scroll-smooth CSS.
